neighborhoods.name: Ciutat Vella (Old Town)
neighborhoods.description: Historic center with the Silk Exchange (UNESCO), Central Market, and the Cathedral of the Holy Grail
neighborhoods.character: Historic UNESCO, market culture, architectural gems
neighborhoods.character_notes: The historic and commercial heart of Valencia. Most walkable area with best access to cultural attractions, restaurants, and public services.

neighborhoods.name: Ruzafa
neighborhoods.description: Trendy multicultural neighborhood with vintage shops, craft cocktail bars, and the best brunch scene

housing.predominantType: Apartments in traditional and renovated buildings

neighborhoods.character_notes: Established residential area offering a quieter lifestyle while maintaining reasonable access to Valencia's center and services.

neighborhoods.name: Benimaclet
neighborhoods.description: Former village with university influence, street art, community gardens, and affordable housing
neighborhoods.character_notes: More affordable option for budget-conscious retirees. May require a car or longer transit times to reach central amenities.

neighborhoods.name: El Cabanyal / Malvarrosa
neighborhoods.description: Beachfront neighborhoods with colorful tile facades, revitalizing waterfront, and growing expat community

housing.predominantType: Beachfront apartments and renovated tile houses

neighborhoods.character_notes: Area with established international community and expat-oriented services. English is more commonly spoken here than in other neighborhoods.
